human_verification.notes: Reviewed hospital quality operations boundary, QAPI and medical-record source baseline, quality reporting and measure evidence expectations, PHI handling, clinical/patient-safety/payer/coding boundaries, hold/release and CAPA discipline, research summary, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required healthcare-and-care-delivery research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for clinical judgment, patient-safety event determination, sentinel or serious event classification, RCA approval, QAPI committee action, peer review, infection prevention, medical necessity, payer interpretation, authorization approval, medical coding, claim correction, PHI disclosure, legal, financial, executive, and final release decisions.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers normal QAPI evidence review, missing authorization/referral/eligibility, contradictory safety evidence, release bypass refusal, incident escalation, PHI-sensitive request handling, payer and clinical boundary refusal, repeat defects, closure without review support, conflicting policy, tenant terminology, missing systems, CAPA packaging, and coding/revenue routing.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.

required_task_and_boundary_metadata: hold, release, nonconformance, incident, CAPA, or unresolved-risk state
clinical, privacy, payer, coding, legal, financial, and final QA boundaries
tenant_runtime_facts: facility, business unit, service line, program, care setting, jurisdiction, source hierarchy, QAPI charter, incident taxonomy, review workflow, RACI, escalation SLA, and approval matrix
EHR, incident, QAPI, measure abstraction, audit, privacy, case management, utilization review, patient access, payer portal, coding, revenue cycle, claim, denial, and appeal system retrieval hooks

payer, plan, coverage context, provider type, facility type, service dates, authorization status, referral dependencies, eligibility and benefits status, privacy, consent, and minimum-necessary constraints
tenant policy precedence, accreditation posture, peer-review handling, CAPA workflow, quality committee authority, approved distribution groups, and rollback owner
regulated_lane_caveats: Do not imply diagnosis, treatment planning, licensed clinical judgment, patient-safety authority, QAPI committee authority, privacy approval, payer-policy interpretation, coding authority, claim correction, legal advice, financial approval, or final release.
Escalate PHI, clinical risk, payer interpretation, source conflict, and safety-critical questions that change the answer.
Preserve hold status when evidence, review support, owner acceptance, or audit trail is missing or contradictory.
orchestrator_return_conditions: required tenant facts, source records, authority context, or current public source context are missing and would change disposition
public, tenant, payer, accreditation, state, contract, or facility policy materially conflicts
the request requires clinical, privacy, payer, coding, legal, financial, safety-critical, committee, or executive authority
the correct adjacent specialist or accountable reviewer cannot be identified

notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
